# NotaryChain

> NotaryChain is an AI-powered online notarization and blockchain escrow platform.
> Documents are scanned by AI forensics for tampering, signers are biometrically
> identity-proofed (photo ID + face match + knowledge-based authentication), and
> every notarial seal is cryptographically anchored on the Hedera public
> blockchain — producing court-admissible, tamper-evident records. The platform is
> Florida Remote Online Notarization (RON) compliant and expanding across U.S. states.

## What NotaryChain does
- Remote Online Notarization (RON): notarize documents online over secure video, in minutes.
- AI document forensics: GPT-powered vision scans each document for tampering and alteration.
- Biometric identity proofing: government ID verification, selfie/face match, and 5-question KBA.
- Blockchain sealing: each seal's SHA-256 hash is anchored to the Hedera Consensus Service for public, tamper-evident verification.
- Smart Document Studio: AI drafting, clause library, compliance checks, and one-click notarization.
- Digital escrow: self-executing, condition-based escrow with on-chain settlement.
- Public verification: anyone can verify a document or certificate by its hash, free.
- Notary directory & trust badge: discover commissioned notaries and display a verifiable trust badge.

## Key questions answered
- How do I notarize a document online? Create a request, upload the document, complete identity proofing (ID + selfie + KBA), and meet the commissioned notary over secure video; the signed document is sealed and anchored on-chain.
- Is online notarization legal? Yes — Remote Online Notarization is legally recognized; NotaryChain is built to Florida RON standards and follows state-by-state requirements.
- How is a NotaryChain document verified? Each document's cryptographic hash is recorded on the Hedera public ledger; anyone can confirm authenticity and integrity at /verify without an account.
- What makes it tamper-evident? Any change to a sealed document changes its hash, which no longer matches the on-chain record — instantly exposing tampering.
